# Set up Bitbucket scanning

Vault Radar allows you to connect to any workspaces on Bitbucket Cloud and any projects on Bitbucket Server.

There are two ways to set up scanning for Bitbucket -

- Import a single Bitbucket workspace or project as a data source.

- Import many Bitbucket workspaces or projects together using a data source host.

To import a single Bitbucket workspace or project as a data source, click **Settings**, then click **Data Sources**.
![Import single Bitbucket workspace or project as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source.png)

To import many Bitbucket workspaces or projects together as a data source host, click **Settings**, then click **Data Source Hosts**.
![Import many Bitbucket workspaces or projects as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source-host.png)

Additionally, for either option, you can choose to scan with [**HCP Vault Radar Scan**](#scan-with-hcp-vault-radar-scan)
or with the [**Vault Radar Agent Scan**](#scan-with-vault-radar-agent-scan).

# Connect to Bitbucket

For either the data source or data source host option, scanning can be set up using **HCP Vault Radar Scan** or using **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

## Prerequisites

Depending on whether you are connecting to Bitbucket Cloud or Bitbucket Server, there are different ways of generating
credentials to access the workspaces or projects.

### Bitbucket Cloud credentials

- Workspace ID
    - Navigate to your Bitbucket Cloud workspace, and the workspace ID should show up in the URL
        - For example, in `https://bitbucket.org/my-workspace/workspace/overview`, the workspace ID is `my-workspace`
        - You can also find this by clicking the gear icon in the upper right corner
          "Settings" (take the "Workspace ID", not the "Workspace name")

        ![Bitbucket Cloud Workspace Settings](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-cloud-workspace-settings.png)

- Token creator email address

- [API token with the right set of scopes](https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-account/docs/manage-api-tokens-for-your-atlassian-account/)
    - read:account
    - read:me
    - read:project:bitbucket
    - read:pullrequest:bitbucket
    - read:repository:bitbucket
    - read:user:bitbucket
    - read:webhook:bitbucket
    - read:workspace:bitbucket
    - write:pullrequest:bitbucket
    - write:webhook:bitbucket

### Bitbucket Server credentials

- Project key
- Bitbucket account name
- HTTP access token (with the right set of permissions)

![Configure Bitbucket Server](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-server-http-access-token.png)

## Scan with HCP Vault Radar Scan

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select either **Bitbucket Cloud** or **Bitbucket Server**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, different input text fields may appear.

### Bitbucket Cloud input

![Bitbucket Cloud scanned by HCP Vault Radar Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-cloud-hcp-data-source.png)

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources**, enter the workspace ID in the **Workspace ID** text field.

1. Enter the email address of the Bitbucket Cloud user that created the API token in the **Enter your Bitbucket token creator email** text field.

1. Enter the API token value in the **Enter your Bitbucket API token** text field.

### Bitbucket Server input

1. Enter the domain URL in the **Enter your Bitbucket domain name** text field.
    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `bitbucket.acme.com`

    ![Bitbucket Server scanned by HCP Vault Radar Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-server-hcp-data-source.png)

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources**, enter your project key in the **Enter your Bitbucket project key** text field.

1. Enter the Bitbucket account name in the **Enter your Bitbucket account** text field.

1. Enter the HTTP access token in the **Enter your Bitbucket HTTP access token** text field.

## Scan with Vault Radar Agent Scan

1. Make sure that the agent pool has been set up, refer to the [agent pool documentation](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) for more information.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**, then select either **Bitbucket Cloud** or **Bitbucket Server**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, different input text fields may appear.

### Bitbucket Cloud input

![Bitbucket Cloud scanned by Vault Radar Agent Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-cloud-agent-data-source.png)

1. Create an environment variable for the API token in the agent environment. This should be in the format `token_creator_email:api_token`.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export BITBUCKET_TOKEN=myemail@example.com:mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Enter the name of the environment variable in the **Enter environment path to your Bitbucket API token** text field with the `env://` prefix.

    **Example:**

    ```plaintext
    env://BITBUCKET_TOKEN
    ```

1. Click **Next**.

### Bitbucket Server input

![Bitbucket Server scanned by Vault Radar Agent Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-server-agent-data-source.png)

1. Create an environment variable for the API token in the agent environment. This should be in the format `account_name:http_access_token`.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export BITBUCKET_TOKEN=acmeuser:mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Enter the name of the environment variable in the **Enter environment path to your Bitbucket HTTP access token** text field with the `env://` prefix.

    **Example:**

    ```plaintext
    env://BITBUCKET_TOKEN
    ```

1. Click **Next**.

# Finish data source import

1. Choose which repositories to monitor by selecting either the **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**

    Monitor active repositories up to limit ![Add Bitbucket all active](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-all-active-resources.png)

    Monitor selected repositories ![Add Bitbucket custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-custom-select-resources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

# Finish data source host import

1. Choose which data sources to monitor by selecting either the **All data sources** or **Select data sources**

    Monitor all data sources up to limit ![Add Bitbucket all](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-all-active-data-sources.png)

    Monitor selected data sources ![Add Bitbucket custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-custom-select-data-sources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

# Update data source

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Sources**, and click the three dots to the right

   ![Update monitored resources](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-update-data-source.png)

1. To update the monitored repositories, click **Edit monitored resources**.

1. If you imported the **Data Source** directly, to update the token, click **Edit data source details**.

1. If these data sources were discovered by importing the **Data Source Host**, to update the token, [update the data source host instead](#update-data-source-host)

# Update data source host

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Source Hosts**, and click the three dots to the right

   ![Update monitored data sources](/img/docs/vault-radar/bitbucket/bitbucket-update-data-source-host.png)

1. To update the monitored data sources, click **Edit data sources**

1. To update the token, click **Edit data source host details**
